mechanism_narrative: The current very low federal funds rate (0.14%) is inconsistent with the economy's potential growth trajectory, so forward-looking markets price in a normalization of monetary policy over the next 20 trading days, causing the 10-year yield to rise.
failure_condition: If the 10-year yield falls or remains flat in more than 30% of 20-day windows following low FEDFUNDS readings (below 1%), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points signals tightening labor market conditions, which compels investors to anticipate higher nominal growth and inflation, pushing 10-year yields higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition the 10-year yield falls in more than 40% of cases over a 20-day window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate broad-based economic expansion, which raises real rate expectations and leads to higher 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If after both series show positive monthly growth the 10-year yield declines in more than 50% of 20-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI level exceeds its 12-month moving average, it signals that inflation is running above trend; further increases in CPI then indicate persistent inflation, pushing bond yields up over a 60-trading-day horizon. In the opposite regime (CPI below MA), increases are viewed as transitory and would be expected to lower yields.
failure_condition: If in the contra-regime (CPI below MA) the 10-year yield also rises after CPI increases in more than 50% of 60-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts signals strengthening residential investment demand, which boosts economic growth expectations and pushes 10-year yields higher over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If monthly increases in HOUST lead to 10-year yield declines in more than 50% of 5-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month growth in retail sales exceeding 0.5% signals robust consumer spending, which raises expectations for nominal GDP and pushes 10-year yields up over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition the 10-year yield declines in more than 40% of 20-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate signals that the economy is recovering without immediate policy tightening, leading investors to anticipate higher future rates and pushing 10-year yields up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2 percentage points AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: If after the joint condition the 10-year yield falls in more than 30% of 20-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth (annualized) exceeds 2.5%, the economy is operating above potential, which creates inflationary pressures and pushes 10-year yields higher over a 60-trading-day horizon. In the opposite regime (growth below 2.5%), the signal reverses and yields are expected to fall.
failure_condition: If in the opposite regime (growth below 2.5%) the 10-year yield also rises in more than 50% of 60-day windows, the hypothesis is rejected.
